session_work: ML store scraping — scraped 69 laptops from PC Center, TuNotebook, EXO, Apple (API fallback), Gfast brand search, and 5 specific product URLs

completed_tasks.name: Verified DB state: 122 laptops loaded (all batches complete), 23 red-labeled, 81 profiles with 0 laptop_ids
Fetch 5 specific product URLs (Lenovo IdeaPad Slim3 Ryzen7, HP 255 G10 x2, Gfast N-758R, N-536R)
Scrape PC Center Computers tienda (29 laptops: Lenovo, HP, Gfast, Dell)
Scrape TuNotebook TechStore tienda (27 laptops: HP, Asus, Acer, MSI, Dell, Lenovo)
Apple MacBooks via products/search API fallback (3 MacBook Air M3)
Gfast brand search via API (14 Gfast models)
Deduplicate + filter $0 price and accessories → 69 clean laptops saved to /tmp/new_laptops_scraped.json

remaining_tasks.name: User reviews /tmp/new_laptops_scraped.json (69 laptops) and approves import
Import approved laptops to Supabase via mcp__supabase__execute_sql (INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING)
Fix seed.sql:303 'macOS Sequoia' → 'macOS' then run DO block for profile-to-laptop assignments (81 profiles)
Frontend: add red badge for availability_warning=true laptops on CatalogCard

blockers.description: Apple official store not scrapeable — listado.mercadolibre.com.ar redirects to account verification; tienda page uses JS rendering without catalog product IDs
blockers.workaround: Used products/search API fallback → 3 MacBook Air M3 from third-party resellers. DB already has 10 Apple laptops. Acceptable for MVP.

blockers.description: Pygcomexarg has no functional tienda page (bare storefront)
blockers.workaround: Their Gfast products covered via PC Center store + Gfast brand search.

human_actions_pending.action: Review /tmp/new_laptops_scraped.json and decide which laptops to import to DB
human_actions_pending.context: 69 laptops scraped. Preview: python3 -c "import json; [print(l['brand'], int(l['price']/1000), 'k', l['name'][:55]) for l in json.load(open('/tmp/new_laptops_scraped.json'))['laptops_flat']]"

decisions.rationale: listado.mercadolibre.com.ar redirects to account verification; /sites/MLA/search blocked for client_credentials

decisions.decision: Extract 7-9 digit MLA IDs from HTML as catalog product IDs → /products/{id} API
decisions.rationale: Only /products/{id} and /products/{id}/items work with client_credentials; /items/{id} blocked (403)

decisions.decision: products/search API fallback for Apple (tienda returns 0 catalog IDs)
decisions.rationale: Apple tienda uses JS rendering, only 10-digit item IDs appear in HTML

decisions.decision: Filter $0 price and accessory keywords post-scraping
decisions.rationale: Many catalog products have no active items → $0; accessories (bags, etc.) slip through laptop keyword filter

ephemeral_files: /tmp/new_laptops_scraped.json — 69 scraped laptops (EPHEMERAL: lost on reboot, regenerate with: python3 scripts/ml_scrape_new.py)
next_action: 1. If /tmp/new_laptops_scraped.json missing: run python3 scripts/ml_scrape_new.py (~3 min). 2. User reviews JSON. 3. Import to Supabase. 4. Fix seed.sql line ~303: change 'macOS Sequoia' to 'macOS'. 5. Run DO block from seed.sql:269 via mcp__supabase__execute_sql to assign 5 laptops to each of 81 profiles.
context_notes: DB state: 122 laptops (all batches from prev session complete — HANDOFF was stale), 23 red-labeled, 81 profiles with 0 laptop_ids. Profile DO block is the critical missing piece for recommendation engine to work. The OS mismatch bug ('macOS Sequoia' vs 'macOS') must be fixed before running it or macos profiles will use the global fallback instead of macOS-specific laptops.
